name: Israel
summary: Day 86 of Hormuz blockade (Sunday May 24). **GAMLIEL (LIKUD, NATIONAL SECURITY CABINET) PUBLICLY CONFIRMED ISRAEL 'WAIT-AND-SEE' POSTURE ON ARMY RADIO — CABINET TONE IS WATCH AND VERIFY, NOT OPERATIONAL PREP. NETANYAHU TOLD TRUMP IN SATURDAY-NIGHT CALL ISRAEL 'WILL CONTINUE TO TAKE MILITARY ACTION AS IT SEES FIT, IRRESPECTIVE OF ANY US-IRAN PEACE DEAL' (TOI SOURCES) — DOMESTIC HARDLINE POSITIONING WITHOUT OPERATIONAL PREPARATION. TRUMP'S PRIVATE ASSURANCE TO NETANYAHU: 'WILL NOT SIGN ANY FINAL AGREEMENT WITHOUT IRAN DISMANTLING ENTIRE NUCLEAR PROGRAM AND REMOVING ALL ENRICHED URANIUM' — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS US ADMIN PUBLIC 'IN PRINCIPLE AGREED TO DISPOSAL.' NO HAARETZ/CHANNEL 12/13 IMMINENT-STRIKE REPORTING REPRODUCED SUNDAY. ISRAELI UNILATERAL-RISK TRIGGER DAY 3 OF 14 (BY 2026-06-05) — OPERATIONAL FLOOR HOLDS.** (1) **Gamliel (Likud, Netanyahu's national security cabinet) on Army Radio Sunday** (CONFIRMED — Yahoo/AP, Inquirer, Times of Israel): Israel taking a 'wait-and-see' approach. First on-record cabinet-level public articulation of Israeli posture since the leaked deal text. (2) **Netanyahu Saturday-night call with Trump (REPORTED — TOI sources)**: he told Trump Israel 'will continue to take military action as it sees fit, irrespective of any US-Iran peace deal' and explicitly emphasised 'freedom of action against threats in all arenas, including Lebanon.' Netanyahu's public position: 'remained unchanged: Iran will not have nuclear weapons' with explicit demand for dismantlement of enrichment sites. (3) **Trump-Netanyahu private call assurance (REPORTED — TOI sources)**: Trump told Netanyahu he 'will not sign any final agreement without the conditions that Iran dismantle its entire nuclear program and remove all enriched uranium from the country.' **This private assurance directly contradicts the US admin's public framing that Iran has 'in principle agreed' to disposal — either Trump is double-tracking with Netanyahu vs the press, or the admin source over-stated Iranian acceptance.** (4) **No operational preparation by IDF, no Haaretz/Channel 12/13 imminent-strike reporting reproduced May 24, no movement on Riklin-claimed 'revealed plans'** — the Israeli unilateral kinetic trigger (Day 3 of 14, by June 5) remains `watching` with operational floor intact. (5) **Israeli media is moving from 'Bibi's hair on fire' framing (May 21-22) toward 'watch and verify' posture** — neither escalating to operational preparation nor accommodating the US deal narrative. This is more dangerous than the prior framing because it preserves Israel's optionality without forcing a public commitment. (6) **Issacharoff thesis (Times of Israel chief military analyst) has NOT publicly pivoted** from 'Iran's nuclear infrastructure remains largely intact underground' — but no escalation toward unilateral-action prep argument today. (7) **Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Day 10 of 45 extension** intact; Pentagon-led security track May 29 launch on calendar; no second IDF KIA inside Litani-area scope (May 14 Negev Dagan remains the only ceasefire-window casualty). PRIOR (May 23): NETANYAHU CHAIRED SECOND SECURITY CABINET MEETING ON IRAN ACROSS THE WEEK BUT ISRAELI OFFICIALS PUBLICLY SAY 'NO DECISION ON IRAN ATTACK; ISRAEL, US IN TUNE ON STRATEGIC CHALLENGES' — TIMES OF ISRAEL EXPLICITLY CONTRADICTS OPERATIONAL-READINESS FRAMING. SINGLE-SOURCE-ANCHOR RIKLIN TV CLAIM OF 'REVEALED CONFIDENTIAL PLANS' UNCORROBORATED BY MAINSTREAM ISRAELI PRINT OUTLETS. ANHA/HAWAR NEWS 'NETANYAHU APPROVES ATTACK PLAN' = SINGLE-SOURCE DIASPORA/KURDISH, CLAIMED ONLY.** Across May 21-23: (1) Netanyahu chaired SECOND security cabinet meeting on Iran across the week (per Times of Israel + Al Jazeera May 21-22 + Haaretz May 23). (2) Israeli TV anchor Shimon Riklin appeared on Hebrew-language broadcast to claim he could 'reveal confidential plans' about renewed attack on Tehran including location of 'a uranium storage facility that could be targeted' (REPORTED — single-source via Al Jazeera May 21 referring to Israeli media; uncorroborated by other Israeli outlets). (3) **A separate Times of Israel report ('Israeli official: No decision on Iran attack; Israel, US in tune on strategic challenges') explicitly contradicts the operational-readiness framing** — official Israeli government line is no decision. (4) **The Kurdish-language ANHA/Hawar News headline 'Netanyahu approves Israeli attack plan on Iran' is single-source diaspora/Kurdish-aligned outlet that has not been independently corroborated by mainstream Israeli media (Haaretz, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post) — treated as CLAIMED only, NOT REPORTED.** (5) **Issacharoff thesis (Times of Israel chief military analyst) has not pivoted from his 'Iran's nuclear infrastructure remains intact underground' framing** — strengthens with Al Arabiya text's omission of nuclear dismantlement but Issacharoff has NOT amplified unilateral-action prep argument today. (6) **Haaretz May 23 confirms US deal-track activity dominates the Israeli media agenda, not unilateral-action prep**. (7) **Israeli unilateral-risk trigger Day 2 of 14 (by 2026-06-05)**: posture hardening rhetorically but operational floor holds — same pattern as Houthi Haifa rhetoric (rhetoric-without-action). (8) **Al Jazeera continues 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire' framing into May 23** — Qatari editorial alignment + Haaretz May 23 confirming US deal-track dominance = the dominant Israeli media frame today is that Israel is WATCHING the US deal track move, not preparing its own kinetic action. This is structurally a notable shift from the May 21-22 'Bibi's hair on fire' + Al Jazeera 'Israel pushes for war' framing — the operational evidence of Israeli unilateral preparation has not surfaced even as rhetorical frustration is on the record. (9) **Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Day 9 of 45**: extension architecture (Pentagon-led security track starting May 29) operationally intact. **The May 22 framing holds**: Israeli political establishment frustration is broad enough to leak (Axios 'hair on fire,' Jerusalem Post echo), but no formal cabinet approval of unilateral action confirmed, and Israeli official rebuttal exists. PRIOR (May 22): **TRUMP-NETANYAHU 'DIFFICULT' MAY 20 CALL — SOURCES DESCRIBED NETANYAHU'S 'HAIR WAS ON FIRE' AFTER (AXIOS SCOOP MAY 20). TRUMP PUBLICLY: NETANYAHU 'WILL DO WHATEVER I WANT HIM TO DO.' THE CLEANEST PUBLICLY STATED US-ISRAEL DIVERGENCE OF THE CYCLE. CNN POLITICS MAY 20: 'TRUMP AND NETANYAHU DIVERGE ON IRAN WAR'S FUTURE IN TENSE PHONE CALL.'** Across May 20-22: (1) **Axios scoop (May 20, Barak Ravid byline)**: Trump-Netanyahu held 'difficult' phone call about Iran ceasefire talks; sources described Netanyahu's 'hair was on fire' after — Netanyahu told Trump he believed delaying expected attacks was a mistake and that the president should continue as planned. (2) **Trump May 20 White House (Times of Israel May 20 liveblog primary, CBS, CNN)**: 'in no hurry' to reach a deal; Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do'; 'open to a limited Iran deal that would only open the Strait of Hormuz and extend the ceasefire.' **This is structurally larger than May 18 Haaretz 'As Trump Hesitates' framing because Trump explicitly subordinated Netanyahu ON THE RECORD.** (3) **CNN Politics May 20: 'Trump and Netanyahu diverge on Iran war's future in tense phone call'** — explicit divergence framing from Democrat-aligned outlet. (4) **Al Jazeera May 21 (Qatari editorial alignment): 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire, but its options may be limited'** — Gulf framing of Israel as deal-spoiler. **Netanyahu reportedly chaired a SECOND security cabinet meeting on Iran across the week** (per Iran International May 19, Al Jazeera May 21, Times of Israel May 20-22) with 'strong desire in the upper echelons of the Israeli government for renewed military action and mounting frustration that Trump is continuing to allow what they say is Iran's diplomatic foot-dragging.' (5) **Issacharoff thesis (Times of Israel chief military analyst) has NOT publicly pivoted from his 'Iran's nuclear infrastructure remains intact underground' framing** — that framing is strengthened by the leaked Al Arabiya deal text's omission of nuclear dismantlement (B1 intel), providing the publicly-stated Israeli intelligence case against accepting the Pakistan-mediated text. (6) **Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Day 8 of 45**: IDF airstrikes targeted Nabatieh (May 22) and al-Haniyya (May 21) per Haaretz wire imagery. Lebanese health ministry casualty tracking continues from prior week's 18-killed/124-wounded reading. NO second IDF KIA across May 19-22 — May 14 Negev Dagan KIA remains the only ceasefire-window casualty inside Litani-area scope. Hezbollah has NOT publicly claimed retaliatory mortar/rocket fire May 19-22. Ceasefire extension architecture (Pentagon-led security track starting May 29) operationally intact. **The most consequential signal**: Israeli media is no longer running unified anti-deal coverage — Jerusalem Post and Axios primary-source the 'Bibi's hair was on fire' framing (both right-of-center outlets willing to run unflattering Netanyahu framing reveals political establishment frustration is now broad enough to leak). **This means either Israeli unilateral kinetic action is being prepared as fact (Haaretz 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares' pattern at higher amplitude), OR the Israeli establishment is operationally accepting a deal it loses on.** Trigger added: 'Israel unilateral kinetic action within US-postponement window or in response to deal text leak (by 2026-06-05).' PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **A3 TRUMP-NETANYAHU PHONE CALL SUNDAY + ISRAELI SECURITY CABINET MEETING + 'CLOCK IS TICKING' TRUMP POST + TUESDAY MAY 19 NSC MEETING SCHEDULED** — Day 1 of 6 remaining in NYT 'as soon as next week' window. Trump and Netanyahu spoke by phone Sunday May 17 ahead of limited Israeli security cabinet meeting; both sides framed call as discussion of 'renewing the war with Iran.' Netanyahu addressed Cabinet: **'our eyes are also open' regarding Iran, 'prepared for any scenario.'** Small security cabinet attendees: FM Sa'ar, Defense Min Katz, Finance Min Smotrich, NSC Min Ben Gvir, Shas chair Deri = war-decision quorum. Trump posted 'for Iran, the clock is ticking' on Truth Social later Sunday (CONFIRMED RHETORIC). Trump expected to meet top national security team Tuesday May 19 (REPORTED via Haaretz/Times of Israel May 18; no White House public announcement through Monday morning IST). **Haaretz May 18 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares for Renewed Iran Strikes' is the cleanest publicly stated US-Israel-divergence framing of the cycle** — Israeli defense establishment positioning for unilateral preparation while Trump described as hesitating despite NYT preparation reporting and his own escalatory rhetoric. Haaretz with strongest IDF military sourcing — framing at minimum the senior IDF reading; at maximum a deliberate Israeli leak intended to *pressure* Trump toward action. **A former senior Israeli intelligence officer in Haaretz piece publicly argues Iran's nuclear infrastructure and missile production remain 'largely intact underground'** — deliberate argument for renewed kinetic action with the strategic concession that Iran may now conclude weaponization is the only deterrent. **A8 ISRAEL-LEBANON DAY 4 OF 45-DAY CEASEFIRE EXTENSION**: Lebanese health ministry reports 18 killed + 124 wounded in Israeli strikes past day (Haaretz live blog May 17, ms.now); cumulative ~2,987 dead / ~9,236 wounded since March 2 (additive). **IDF reported NO new injuries through Monday morning IST; no second IDF KIA confirmed.** Litani-induced Israeli-soldier-KIA trigger remains `active_fired` from Day 2 of 14 (Dagan mortar killing); May 16 'second IDF KIA in southern Lebanon during 45-day ceasefire extension window' trigger remains UNTRIPPED. Both sides continue to operate within unwritten kinetic floor (strikes) and ceiling (no escalation to mass-casualty IDF events). **Pentagon security track May 29 launch remains on calendar.** Al Jazeera continues scare-quoted 'ceasefire' framing on Israel-Lebanon. **The dominant Israeli media tone is operational urgency: this is the strike window, and US hesitation is the problem.** **PRIOR (May 17): KINETIC AXIS HARDENS PUBLICLY**: NYT (Friday May 15 / Saturday May 16, citing two Middle Eastern officials) REPORTED that **Israel and the US are carrying out 'most intense preparations yet to renew attacks on Iran, possibly as soon as next week'** — options include (i) commando operation to extract nuclear material believed buried under rubble at struck nuclear sites; (ii) ground forces to seize Kharg Island. Times of Israel May 16 liveblog led with 'As Iran talks stall, Israel and US prepping to renew war as soon as next week — report' as its dominant geopolitical headline. **Israeli media operationally framing the kinetic option as live and imminent, not hypothetical.** Haaretz consistent with NYT preparation framing — Israeli media bellwether for IDF senior-officer thinking. Jerusalem Post amplified 'US-Israel Iran attacks could resume next week' (article 896353). Israel Hayom likely supportive per recent pattern. Eurovision runner-up coverage interleaved with the war stories — domestic-political pivot signal that the cabinet wants the architectural-extension framing to land softer than the kinetic-preparation framing. **ISRAEL-LEBANON CEASEFIRE DAY 3 OF 45-DAY EXTENSION**: On Saturday May 16, IDF conducted strikes 'throughout the day on several areas in southern Lebanon' targeting what it called Hezbollah infrastructure — Tayr Falsayh (Nabatiyeh), Yanouh, Shahabiya, Qaqaiyat al-Sanoubar (Sidon district), Tuffahta, Ghassaniya, and Kawthariyat al-Sayyad (Al Jazeera May 16 'Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon continue despite ceasefire extension' — scare-quotes positioning architecture as nominal). On Sunday morning May 17, **Hezbollah fired several rockets and explosive drones at IDF troops in southern Lebanon** (CONFIRMED — IDF statement, Times of Israel May 17 liveblog primary). Rockets and several drones were shot down; some drones exploded near Israeli forces; **NO INJURIES — IDF explicitly stated.** One drone triggered sirens in border community Misgav Am. **The Litani-induced-Israeli-KIA trigger that fired Day 2 of 14 (Dagan May 14) now at DAY 4 OF 14 inside the 45-day extension window; the 'second IDF KIA in southern Lebanon during 45-day extension' trigger added May 16 remains UNTRIPPED.** Both sides operating within unwritten kinetic floor and ceiling under formal ceasefire: Hezbollah striking IDF without killing IDF; IDF striking Hezbollah infrastructure without conducting Beirut-suburb operations. **The architecture-collapse scenario has been further deferred — the cabinet's choice to extend rather than harden was validated by 48 hours of no second IDF KIA despite continued kinetic exchange.** Lebanese health ministry reported 2,969 cumulative dead and 9,112 wounded since March 2. **PRIOR (May 16):** ISRAEL-LEBANON CEASEFIRE EXTENDED 45 DAYS to ~2026-06-29 with PENTAGON-LED SECURITY TRACK to begin May 29** (CONFIRMED — US State Department primary May 15, Bloomberg, PBS, Times of Israel liveblog, CGTN, Euronews, India TV, CNBC, Foreign Policy). State Department spokesperson Tommy Pigott called the talks 'highly productive.' **The cabinet, faced yesterday with the choice between (a) extending under conditions where Hezbollah just killed a Golani Brigade soldier near the Litani and (b) hardening and risking architectural collapse, CHOSE (a) WITH US BACKING.** The architecture-collapse scenario the brief warned of has been **deferred, not foreclosed**. Pentagon-led security architecture beginning May 29 substantively elevates the process from State-led ambassadorial to Pentagon-led security architecture — material structural anchoring. **THE LITANI-INDUCED ISRAELI KIA TRIGGER continues to count down at DAY 3 OF 14** — the ceasefire extension does not retroactively un-fire the trigger. Critically: **NO SECOND IDF KIA IN SOUTHERN LEBANON SINCE DAGAN MAY 14, per available reporting through May 16 morning** — the 14-day window's worst-case (second KIA collapses the architecture) has not materialised in the first 48h. Hezbollah's 24-attacks-in-24-hours pattern (Al Jazeera May 14) has not been re-reported with a fresh KIA. **i24News reported Netanyahu's Jerusalem Day Ammunition Hill remarks 'the regime of terror in Iran is weaker than ever, the State of Israel is stronger than ever'** — coalition rhetoric absorbing the diplomatic-extension narrative as a position of strength rather than weakness. **Israel Hayom (coalition-aligned) finally editorialised on the extension — supportive but with hawkish conditioning on Hezbollah disarmament** — closing the 'internal-dispute absence' yesterday's brief flagged. Times of Israel led with the ceasefire-extension story as the structural positive of the day; Haaretz framed the Pentagon-security-track May 29 as the substantive elevation. **The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extension is the day's single largest de-escalation signal, layered against the cleanly bearish US-equity/Treasury session (S&P -1.24%, 10Y +14bp to 4.595%) driven by Trump's reversal + new May 16 nuclear ultimatum letter to Iran, and against the new Saudi Helsinki-Accords non-aggression pact proposal floated FT May 14.** Cabinet-vs-establishment-vs-IDF triangle now stress-tested but the architecture held. **A3 ISRAEL-LEBANON WASHINGTON TALKS DAY 2 CLOSED** with Pigott 'highly productive' framing. PRIOR (May 15): **A4 LITANI-INDUCED ISRAELI KIA TRIGGER FIRED ON DAY 2 OF 14 WINDOW** (`watching` → **`active_fired`**): Staff Sgt. Negev Dagan, 20, of the Golani Brigade's 12th Battalion, from Moshav Dekel in the northwestern Negev, was killed by Hezbollah mortar fire in southern Lebanon on May 14 (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel primary, JNS, i24News, Haaretz, Israeli MoD primary). IDF named the soldier and confirmed location near the Litani river, where Golani's reconnaissance unit has been conducting the weeklong raid disclosed yesterday. Total IDF war toll now 937 per JNS. **Yesterday's brief explicitly added the 'Litani-induced Israeli KIA within 14 days' trigger at Day 1 of 14; that trigger has fired on Day 2 — earlier and cleaner than median window suggested.** Political consequence inside Israel is significant: Washington Israel-Lebanon talks Day 2 closing today simultaneously, ceasefire extension expected, cabinet now faces choice between (a) extending ceasefire under conditions where Hezbollah just inflicted KIA on uniformed Golani Brigade soldier in IDF-disclosed territory beyond the river, or (b) hardening response and risking collapse of the Washington architecture. **The cabinet-vs-establishment-vs-IDF triangle is now under direct operational stress.** Hezbollah launched 24 attacks on IDF positions, soldiers and military vehicles in southern Lebanon over a 24-hour window per Al Jazeera; the Dagan KIA is one consequence of that escalation. **A3 ISRAEL-LEBANON WASHINGTON TALKS DAY 2 CLOSED; STATE DEPARTMENT CHARACTERIZED AS 'PRODUCTIVE AND POSITIVE'** (Haaretz May 15 liveblog primary). Lebanese delegation under Simon Karam pressed for Israeli troop withdrawal timeline and Lebanese-prisoner release; Israeli delegation under Ron Dermer maintained no immediate withdrawal plans and prioritized Hezbollah disarmament. **Critical date correction: April-23-extended ceasefire expires 'early hours of Sunday' — May 17, NOT May 15 as yesterday's brief recorded.** Sources pre-positioning expectations for an 'extended political process' announcement under US sponsorship; Reuters/Manila Times reporting that statement will announce a ceasefire extension. Lebanese Health Ministry reported 512 Lebanese killed by Israeli strikes since April 17 ceasefire implementation (Al Jazeera). Israeli framing (JPost, JNS, Haaretz) emphasizes Hezbollah disarmament; Lebanese framing (Al Jazeera, Madamsr) emphasizes 512 dead and continued Israeli operations beyond Litani. Times of Israel and JNS led with Dagan KIA as the structural event of the day; Haaretz framed State Department's 'productive and positive' line against Israeli reality on the ground; Ynet leaning into human-cost angle; Israel Hayom (Netanyahu-coalition-aligned) has not yet editorialised on whether to extend or harden — absence is itself a signal of internal coalition dispute. **Trump-Xi joint statement that Iran 'can never have a nuclear weapon' substantively reinforces de-escalation rhetorical track** but Netanyahu's CBS 'not over until uranium removed' demand structure unchanged. PRIOR (May 14): IDF 'weeklong raid' beyond Litani led by Golani Brigade's reconnaissance unit; KIA trigger added at Day 1 of 14. — meaningful UPGRADE of yesterday's 'first publicly-acknowledged Litani crossing': the IDF itself is now framing the operation as a sustained week-long campaign, not a one-off crossing** (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel liveblog May 13, JNS, Haaretz). Killed 'some 15 Hezbollah operatives' in past 24 hours; 'some 45 Hezbollah targets' total over 24 hours including command centres, surveillance posts, weapon depots. **NO new Israeli soldier KIA in past 24 hours — the new 'Litani-induced Israeli KIA within 14 days' trigger (added yesterday) is at Day 2 of 14 and HAS NOT activated.** Hezbollah fired explosive drones at Israel May 13 (Haaretz May 13 liveblog); drones triggered an Iron Dome interceptor over southern Lebanon that the IDF later said was a false alarm. Lebanese press reports 13 killed in southern Lebanon strikes (Jibsheet, Kfar Dounine, Nabatieh) including a child, woman, Lebanese army member; two Israeli soldiers wounded (CNN). **STATE DEPARTMENT CONFIRMED PRIMARY: third round of US-facilitated Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington May 14-15, Lebanese delegation led by Simon Karam, Israeli delegation led by Ron Dermer, agenda explicitly including 'complete disarmament of Hezbollah' alongside 'full restoration of Lebanese state authority' = material structural anchoring. The April-23-extended 3-week ceasefire expires around May 15, exactly as the talks finish — the timing is choreographed.** Ceasefire status verbal: Zamir's 'no ceasefire' line carries forward; coalition-vs-establishment-vs-IDF triangle continues. Haaretz 'U.S. Vice President Vance Says Progress Is Being Made in Iran Talks' May 13 ran the Vance softer line as lede, juxtaposing it against May 11 cabinet/Netanyahu hardening — Haaretz framing Vance as establishment-aligned voice of Washington and Trump as populist-aligned. JPost ran Hezbollah-drone-strike + IDF-tank-strike split coverage; JNS ran IDF weeklong-Litani-raid frame favourably (Golani Brigade Reconnaissance Unit operational pride framing). Cabinet-vs-establishment-vs-IDF triangle widening, Haaretz tilting toward IDF-establishment, JPost/JNS tilting toward coalition. Times of Israel May 14 liveblog leads with 'Iranian FM threatens UAE, claims Tehran knew of Netanyahu's visit to Abu Dhabi' — Israeli press has now operationally integrated the trilateral (Iran-UAE-Israel) framing as a single conflict surface. **PRIOR (May 13 — Litani crossing context): (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel liveblog May 12); IDF Chief of Staff EYAL ZAMIR: 'there is no cease-fire in Lebanon' — most senior IDF officer publicly declaring ceasefire dead, against Netanyahu's prior 'war not over' framing.** Material escalation of ground-tempo beyond air strikes. IDF said operation targeted Hezbollah military structures; explosive drones at troops landed in open areas. Lebanese Health Ministry recorded ≥13 killed in Israeli attacks over prior 24h; cumulative war toll now 2,704 killed / 8,311 wounded (vs 2,869 in yesterday's brief — Lebanese tally appears to draw from a different reference framework). Hezbollah continues precision strikes on IDF gatherings at al-Taybe, Shama, Biyyada, Alman-Al-Qusayr triangle, Khallet al-Raj near Deir Siryan. NO NEW ISRAELI SOLDIER KIA beyond Glovanyov (5th, May 10) — total holds at 5 since ceasefire, despite Litani crossing materially raises casualty + miscalculation risk. CGTN single-source May 12 SAM-at-IAF claim STILL NOT Western-corroborated; remains REPORTED-CLAIMED at best. **Israeli media establishment-vs-coalition gap NOW STRUCTURAL (widening further). Haaretz extended Sunday's 'As Netanyahu's War Narrative Unravels, Iran's Uranium Becomes His Lifeline' with 'With His Iran Deterrence Squandered, Trump Might Feel Forced to Resume War' (May 12) and 'What Netanyahu — and 60 Minutes — Left Out of His Interview' (May 11-12) — opinion-page but operating as most direct centre-left challenge to official war narrative of cycle. Haaretz NEWS desk (Litani-crossing reporting + Hezbollah-fighting-may-continue framing) treats Netanyahu's 'war not over' as deliberate political extension rather than military necessity. Times of Israel + Jerusalem Post ran 60 Minutes line + Litani crossing as IDF-confirmed operations.** Bennett rally framing ('alliance of draft-dodgers is crashing') = domestic political infrastructure organising against the coalition. Coalition stability narrative fraying inside Israeli establishment press while IDF-Hezbollah operational tempo is HARDENING not softening = cabinet-vs-establishment-vs-IDF triangle now visible in coverage. INSS poll (carried May 11): 61% Israelis OPPOSE ceasefire, 73% expect fighting to resume within a year, Jewish opposition to war nearly tripled (4% → 11.5%). **Netanyahu CBS 60 Minutes line carries forward — war 'not over' until uranium removed + enrichment sites dismantled + proxies neutralized — 'you go in and you take it out';** Trump-in-Beijing forces 48h pause window May 14-16. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12):: WAR 'NOT OVER' UNTIL URANIUM REMOVED, ENRICHMENT SITES DISMANTLED, AND PROXIES NEUTRALIZED — 'YOU GO IN AND YOU TAKE IT OUT.'** Said Mojtaba 'alive but holed up in some bunker' — convergent with prior US intel (CNN/KVIA May 8). **Israeli media framing diverges sharply: JPost + ToI ran straight; HAARETZ published 'As Netanyahu's War Narrative Unravels, Iran's Uranium Becomes His Lifeline' — explicit accusation that uranium-removal demand is now domestic political survival, not strategic necessity. Haaretz separately: 'Netanyahu Says Iran War Not Over, Signals Hezbollah Fighting May Continue After Cease-fire' — Haaretz the only outlet treating Hezbollah linkage as DELIBERATE war-extension.** Ynet contrast headline: 'Netanyahu 2025: We destroyed Iran's nuclear facilities; Netanyahu 2026: Sites we still need to neutralize.' **INSS poll backdrop: 61% of Israelis OPPOSE the ceasefire, 73% expect fighting to resume within a year, but Jewish opposition to the war nearly tripled (4% → 11.5%).** **STRUCTURAL READ: Netanyahu publicly raising ceiling on what counts as a US-Iran deal WHILE US publicly rejecting Iran's response — narrowing landing zone from both ends simultaneously.** **A6 HEZBOLLAH EXPLOSIVE DRONE STRIKE NEAR MANARA MAY 10 ~16:00 KILLED IDF RESERVIST WO (RES.) ALEXANDER GLOVANYOV, 47, 6924th Transport Battalion, of Petah Tikva (announced May 11) = 5TH IDF SOLDIER KILLED post-April 16 ceasefire, ALL FIVE LINKED TO 'CHEAP, HARD-TO-DETECT' FPV/EXPLOSIVE DRONES** — but Glovanyov is a SOLDIER killed on border near Manara, NOT a CIVILIAN inside Israel proper; May 10 trigger window for Israeli civilian casualty from Hezbollah EXPIRED UNTRIPPED on May 10, holds through May 11-12 with no civilian casualty. IDF struck 30+ Lebanon locations May 11 (evacuation warnings Rihan, Jarjouaa, Kfar Roummane, Nmairiyeh, Arab Salim, Jmaijmeh, Mashgharah, Qellaya, Harouf); drone strike Zebdine killed 2 civilians distributing bread; airstrike on Abba killed 2/wounded 5; **FIRST BEIRUT SOUTHERN-SUBURB STRIKE SINCE APRIL 16 CEASEFIRE.** Lebanese Health Ministry ≥23 killed prior 24h; cumulative war toll 2,869. May 12: IDF strikes Adshit (3), Haris (1, 7th cumulative), Dabaal — ≥7 killed, 7 wounded (Lebanese NNA). **NEW VARIABLE MAY 12: surface-to-air missile fired from Lebanon at IAF aircraft over south Lebanon; IDF launched interceptor; alerts triggered along Israel's Mediterranean coast (REPORTED — CGTN single-source as of filing; needs Israeli-side corroboration).** Hezbollah claimed 24 attacks May 11 (artillery/drones/missiles at Khiam, Deir Seryan, Tayr Harfa, Bayyada, Rashaf, Naqoura) — Hezbollah-side CLAIMED. **B10 KNESSET EXTENDED EMERGENCY RESERVIST CALL-UP AUTHORITY (up to 400,000) UNTIL MAY 31; IDF Chief Lt. Gen. EYAL ZAMIR WARNED CLOSED-DOOR KNESSET SESSION MILITARY 'NEEDS MORE SOLDIERS IMMEDIATELY' — ~12,000 additional recruits needed mostly combat troops, operational focus IRAN + LEBANON + GAZA.** Reservist exhaustion narrative growing — institutional read: Israeli political-military system is INSTITUTIONALIZING the war footing, not winding down. Reservist morale stress is emerging political variable that CONSTRAINS Netanyahu's 'go in and take it out' posture. **Trump China summit May 14-15 + WH Sit-Room May 11 weighing renewed Project Freedom restart** = forced pause window; Netanyahu's CBS 'not over' line raises pressure on landing zone.

active_triggers.condition: Iran strikes Dimona nuclear reactor directly
Channel 12/13 or Haaretz publishes IDF imminent-strike preparation on Iran track within 72 hours
Second IDF KIA in southern Lebanon during the 45-day ceasefire extension window (by ~2026-06-29) — would test Pentagon-track-May29 architecture
active_triggers.note: Day 3 of 14 Litani-KIA window inside the new 45-day extension. No second KIA in first 48h post-Dagan. Trigger condition specifically: a second KIA collapses the architecture even with the extension in place.

active_triggers.condition: Pentagon security track May 29 produces public framework deliverable within 30 days of launch (by 2026-06-28)
active_triggers.note: Substantively elevates the process from State-led ambassadorial to Pentagon-led security architecture. A public framework deliverable would mark the architecture as durable; absence would suggest extension was political stopgap.

active_triggers.condition: Israeli unilateral kinetic action on Iran within US-postponement window or in response to leaked deal text (by 2026-06-05)
active_triggers.note: Markets analyst E4 proposed. Bibi 'hair on fire' (Axios May 20) + Netanyahu's second security cabinet meeting + Issacharoff 'nuclear infrastructure intact underground' framing + Al Jazeera 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire' framing converge. Cascade if fired: brent +$15-25 gap, gold +$300-500, VIX +8-12 pts, nifty -3-5%. Open-ended deadline of June 5 covers the next 14 days of deal-architecture-vs-Israeli-unilateral risk.

recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP-NETANYAHU 'DIFFICULT' MAY 20 CALL — SOURCES SAID NETANYAHU'S 'HAIR WAS ON FIRE' AFTER (AXIOS SCOOP). TRUMP PUBLICLY: NETANYAHU 'WILL DO WHATEVER I WANT HIM TO DO.' CNN POLITICS: 'TRUMP AND NETANYAHU DIVERGE ON IRAN WAR'S FUTURE.' AL JAZEERA (QATAR): 'ISRAEL PUSHES FOR WAR AMID US CEASEFIRE.' THE CLEANEST PUBLICLY STATED US-ISRAEL DIVERGENCE OF THE CYCLE. CEASEFIRE DAY 8 OF 45 — NABATIEH/AL-HANIYYA STRIKES, NO SECOND IDF KIA.** (1) **Axios scoop May 20 (Barak Ravid byline)**: Trump-Netanyahu had a 'difficult' phone call about Iran ceasefire talks; sources described Netanyahu's 'hair was on fire' after — Netanyahu told Trump delaying expected attacks was a mistake and that the president should continue as planned. (2) **Trump May 20 White House remarks (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — Times of Israel May 20 liveblog primary, CBS News, CNN, The Week)**: 'in no hurry to reach an agreement,' Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do,' 'open to a limited Iran deal that would only open the Strait of Hormuz and extend the ceasefire,' was 'an hour away' from ordering new strikes Monday May 18 before Gulf allies asked him to wait. (3) **CNN Politics May 20: 'Trump and Netanyahu diverge on Iran war's future in tense phone call'** — explicit divergence framing. **Al Jazeera May 21: 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire, but its options may be limited'** — Qatari editorial alignment openly framing Israel as deal-spoiler. (4) **Netanyahu reportedly chaired a SECOND security cabinet meeting on Iran across the week** with 'strong desire in upper echelons of Israeli government for renewed military action and mounting frustration that Trump is continuing to allow what they say is Iran's diplomatic foot-dragging' (Iran International May 19, Al Jazeera May 21, Times of Israel May 20-22). (5) **Issacharoff (Times of Israel chief military analyst) has NOT publicly pivoted from his 'Iran's nuclear infrastructure remains intact underground' framing** — strengthened by the leaked Al Arabiya deal text's omission of nuclear dismantlement (B1 intel). This is the publicly-stated Israeli intelligence case against accepting the Pakistan-mediated text. (6) **Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Day 8 of 45**: IDF airstrikes Nabatieh (May 22) + al-Haniyya (May 21) per Haaretz wire imagery. Lebanese health ministry casualty tracking continues. **NO second IDF KIA across May 19-22** — May 14 Negev Dagan KIA remains the only ceasefire-window casualty inside Litani-area scope. Hezbollah has NOT publicly claimed retaliatory mortar/rocket fire May 19-22. Pentagon-led security track starting May 29 operationally intact. **The most consequential signal — both Jerusalem Post and Axios (both right-of-center) ran the deeply unflattering 'Bibi's hair on fire' framing**: political establishment frustration is now broad enough to leak through coalition-aligned channels. **Two paths**: (a) Israeli unilateral kinetic action is being prepared as fact (Haaretz 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares' pattern at higher amplitude); OR (b) Israeli establishment is operationally accepting a deal it loses on. The trigger 'Israel unilateral kinetic action within US-postponement window or in response to leaked deal text (by 2026-06-05)' added today at watching.

recent_signals.headline: **HOLDING-POSTURE — MINIMAL CONTEMPORANEOUS RESPONSE TO TRUMP'S POSTPONEMENT THROUGH TUESDAY MORNING IST.** Israel Hayom carried the Trump postponement as fact ('Trump delayed Iran strike at Gulf leaders' request') without contemporaneous Israeli cabinet response. Haaretz's May 16 'Trump Hesitates / Israel Prepares' framing remains the contemporaneous position; no new piece updated the framing in light of the postponement. **The Issacharoff (Haaretz May 18) 'Iran's nuclear infrastructure and missile production capabilities remain largely intact underground' framing IS the cleanest publicly-stated Israeli-intelligence argument against the postponement**: every day the postponement extends is a day Iran's underground infrastructure remains operational. **The negative-space-Israeli-response is itself a signal**: the small security cabinet that convened Sunday (Sa'ar, Katz, Smotrich, Ben Gvir, Deri) would normally be expected to issue some coordinated public response within 36 hours of a US strike postponement. Silence preserves leverage for a possible unilateral Israeli action inside the postponement window (NYT 'seven hours warning' framing), but also signals coalition discipline holding so far. **Houthi 'maritime blockade' of Haifa announcement (CONFIRMED RHETORIC, NO ACTION) lands on the same day** — Israeli government and IDF responses not surfaced through Tuesday morning IST.
recent_signals.verification: Israel Hayom postponement coverage = CONFIRMED (primary Israeli outlet); no contemporaneous cabinet response = CONFIRMED ABSENCE; Issacharoff framing = REPORTED (Haaretz sourcing); no IDF response to Houthi-Haifa = CONFIRMED ABSENCE.

recent_signals.headline: **TRUMP-NETANYAHU PHONE CALL SUNDAY + ISRAELI SECURITY CABINET MEETING + TRUMP 'CLOCK IS TICKING' POST + TUESDAY NSC MEETING — Haaretz 'As Trump Hesitates, Israel Prepares' is the cleanest US-Israel divergence frame of cycle.** Trump-Netanyahu phone call Sunday May 17 ahead of limited Israeli security cabinet meeting (FM Sa'ar, Defense Min Katz, Finance Min Smotrich, NSC Min Ben Gvir, Shas chair Deri = war-decision quorum); both sides frame call as 'renewing the war with Iran.' Netanyahu addressed Cabinet: 'our eyes are also open' regarding Iran, 'prepared for any scenario.' Trump 'for Iran, the clock is ticking' Truth Social post Sunday (CONFIRMED RHETORIC). Trump expected to convene NSC Tuesday May 19 to discuss military options (REPORTED via Haaretz May 18; no White House public confirmation through Monday morning IST). **Haaretz May 18 'former senior Israeli intelligence officer'**: Iran's nuclear infrastructure and missile production remain LARGELY INTACT UNDERGROUND; Tehran may now conclude only nuclear weapons can deter future wars — cleanest publicly stated Israeli-intelligence case that strikes-so-far produced weaponization incentive rather than dismantlement; inverts Trump administration's strategic premise. **A8 ISRAEL-LEBANON DAY 4 OF 45-DAY EXTENSION**: Lebanese health ministry reports 18 killed + 124 wounded in past day of Israeli strikes (Haaretz live blog May 17, ms.now); cumulative ~2,987 dead / ~9,236 wounded if applied additively. **IDF reported NO new injuries through Monday morning IST; no second IDF KIA confirmed.** Litani-induced Israeli-soldier-KIA trigger remains active_fired (Dagan May 14); May 16 'second IDF KIA in southern Lebanon during 45-day extension window' trigger remains UNTRIPPED. Both sides operating within unwritten kinetic floor (strikes) and ceiling (no mass-casualty IDF events). Al Jazeera 'ceasefire' in scare quotes; PBS NewsHour and ms.now confirm kinetic continuation; no Hezbollah claim of Israeli mass-casualty event. **Pentagon-led security track scheduled May 29 launch remains on calendar.** **Markets cascade fires Monday**: Brent +2.0% to $111.47 (StanChart $130-145 path activates); USD/INR ₹96.29 record; Sensex/Nifty -1.30%/-1.04%. **The Tuesday NSC operational binary**: (a) strike order → IDF preparedness validated; (b) public back-off → IDF unilateral-preparation framing intensifies; (c) nothing operational → Branch 2 extended-economic-warfare holds.

recent_signals.headline: **ISRAEL-IRAN KINETIC PREPARATION REPORTED + LEBANON CEASEFIRE DAY 3 OF 45-DAY EXTENSION HOLDS DESPITE CONTINUED EXCHANGE.** (1) NYT (Friday May 15 / Saturday May 16, citing two Middle Eastern officials) REPORTED that Israel and the US are carrying out 'most intense preparations yet to renew attacks on Iran, possibly as soon as next week' — options include (i) commando operation to extract nuclear material buried at struck nuclear sites; (ii) ground forces to seize Kharg Island. Times of Israel May 16 liveblog led with 'As Iran talks stall, Israel and US prepping to renew war as soon as next week — report' as its dominant geopolitical headline. Haaretz consistent with NYT preparation framing. Jerusalem Post amplified 'US-Israel Iran attacks could resume next week' (article 896353). **Israeli media operationally framing the kinetic option as live and imminent, not hypothetical.** Eurovision runner-up coverage interleaved with the war stories — cabinet wants the architectural-extension framing to land softer than the kinetic-preparation framing. **NO on-record IDF / Mossad / Defense Ministry confirmation of operation planning.** (2) **LEBANON FRONT — DAY 3 OF 45-DAY EXTENSION**: On Saturday May 16, IDF struck 'throughout the day on several areas in southern Lebanon' (Al Jazeera May 16 'Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon continue despite ceasefire extension') — Tayr Falsayh (Nabatiyeh), Yanouh, Shahabiya, Qaqaiyat al-Sanoubar (Sidon district), Tuffahta, Ghassaniya, Kawthariyat al-Sayyad. Lebanese health ministry: 2,969 cumulative dead and 9,112 wounded since March 2. On Sunday morning May 17, **Hezbollah fired several rockets and explosive drones at IDF troops in southern Lebanon** (CONFIRMED — IDF statement, Times of Israel May 17 liveblog primary). Rockets and several drones shot down; some drones exploded near Israeli forces; **NO INJURIES — IDF explicitly stated.** One drone triggered sirens in border community Misgav Am. **Litani-induced-Israeli-KIA trigger now Day 4 of 14 inside the 45-day extension window; the 'second IDF KIA during 45-day extension' trigger added May 16 remains UNTRIPPED.** **The operational fact that matters most for the trigger map**: Hezbollah is striking IDF without killing IDF; the IDF is striking Hezbollah infrastructure without conducting Beirut-suburb operations. Both sides operating within an unwritten kinetic floor and ceiling under the formal ceasefire. **The architecture-collapse scenario has been further deferred — the cabinet's choice to extend rather than harden was validated by 48 hours of no second IDF KIA despite continued kinetic exchange.**

recent_signals.headline: **ISRAEL-LEBANON CEASEFIRE EXTENDED 45 DAYS to ~2026-06-29 with PENTAGON-LED SECURITY TRACK to begin May 29** (CONFIRMED — US State Department primary May 15, Bloomberg 'Israel, Lebanon Extend Ceasefire 45 Days After Washington Talks,' PBS NewsHour, CGTN, Euronews, Times of Israel liveblog, India TV, CNBC, ms.now, usnews, Foreign Policy). State Department spokesperson Tommy Pigott called the talks 'highly productive.' **The cabinet, faced with the choice between (a) extending under conditions where Hezbollah just killed a Golani Brigade soldier near the Litani and (b) hardening and risking architectural collapse, CHOSE (a) WITH US BACKING.** The architecture-collapse scenario the brief warned of has been **deferred, not foreclosed**. Pentagon-led security architecture beginning May 29 substantively elevates the process from State-led ambassadorial to Pentagon-led security architecture — material structural anchoring. Hezbollah was not a participant in the talks. Lebanese officials hoped negotiations would pave the way for 'tackling thorny issues including the withdrawal of Israeli forces from southern Lebanon and the disarmament of Hezbollah.' Foreign Policy framed it as 'Trump's Lebanon Negotiations Are Breaking the Country' — opposite framing. **The Litani-induced Israeli KIA trigger that fired yesterday Day 2 of 14 CONTINUES TO COUNT DOWN at Day 3 of 14 inside the new 45-day extension window** — the extension does not retroactively un-fire the trigger. Critically: **NO SECOND IDF KIA IN SOUTHERN LEBANON SINCE DAGAN MAY 14 per available reporting through May 16 morning** — the 14-day window's worst-case (second KIA collapses the architecture) has not materialised in the first 48h. Hezbollah's 24-attacks-in-24-hours pattern (Al Jazeera May 14) has not been re-reported with a fresh KIA. **i24News reported Netanyahu's Jerusalem Day Ammunition Hill remarks 'the regime of terror in Iran is weaker than ever, the State of Israel is stronger than ever'** — coalition rhetoric absorbing the diplomatic-extension narrative as a position of strength rather than weakness. **Israel Hayom (coalition-aligned) finally editorialised on the extension — supportive but with hawkish conditioning on Hezbollah disarmament** — closing the 'internal-dispute absence' yesterday's brief flagged. Times of Israel led with the ceasefire-extension story; Haaretz framed the Pentagon-security-track May 29 as the substantive elevation. Trump-side context: Trump immediately reversed post-summit ('decimation' Truth Social + Hannity 'not patient') and on May 16 sent Iran a new nuclear ultimatum letter — Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extension is the single largest de-escalation signal of the day against an otherwise escalating Iran-track and a stagflation-shock US-market session.

NEGEV DAGAN, 20, GOLANI BRIGADE 12TH BATTALION KILLED BY HEZBOLLAH MORTAR FIRE NEAR THE LITANI MAY 14** (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel primary, JNS, i24News, Haaretz, Israeli MoD primary). IDF named the soldier from Moshav Dekel in the northwestern Negev and confirmed location near Litani river, where Golani's reconnaissance unit has been conducting the weeklong raid disclosed yesterday. Total IDF war toll now 937 per JNS. **Yesterday's brief explicitly added the 'Litani-induced Israeli KIA within 14 days' trigger at Day 1 of 14; that trigger has fired on Day 2 — earlier and cleaner than median window suggested.** Hezbollah launched 24 attacks on IDF positions, soldiers and military vehicles in southern Lebanon over a 24-hour window per Al Jazeera; Dagan KIA is one consequence of that escalation. **The political consequence inside Israel is significant** because the Washington talks Day 2 are simultaneously underway and ceasefire extension is the expected outcome — cabinet now faces choice between (a) extending ceasefire under conditions where Hezbollah just inflicted KIA on uniformed Golani Brigade soldier in IDF-disclosed territory beyond the river, or (b) hardening response and risking collapse of Washington architecture. **A3 ISRAEL-LEBANON WASHINGTON TALKS DAY 2 CLOSED; STATE DEPARTMENT CHARACTERIZED AS 'PRODUCTIVE AND POSITIVE'** (Haaretz May 15 liveblog primary). Karam pressed for Israeli withdrawal timeline + Lebanese-prisoner release; Dermer maintained no immediate withdrawal + prioritized Hezbollah disarmament. **CRITICAL DATE CORRECTION: April-23-extended ceasefire expires 'early hours of Sunday' = May 17, NOT May 15 as yesterday's brief recorded.** Reuters/Manila Times reporting statement will announce ceasefire extension. Lebanese Health Ministry: 512 Lebanese killed by Israeli strikes since April 17 ceasefire implementation (Al Jazeera). Israeli media: Times of Israel + JNS led with Dagan KIA as structural event; Haaretz framed State Department 'productive and positive' line against Israeli reality on ground; Ynet leaning into human-cost angle; **Israel Hayom (Netanyahu-coalition-aligned) has NOT yet editorialised on whether to extend or harden** — absence is itself a signal of internal coalition dispute. **Trump-Xi joint statement that Iran 'can never have a nuclear weapon' substantively reinforces de-escalation rhetorical track** but Netanyahu's CBS 'not over until uranium removed' demand structure unchanged.

Lebanese press: 13 killed in southern Lebanon strikes (Jibsheet, Kfar Dounine, Nabatieh) including child, woman, Lebanese army member; 2 Israeli soldiers wounded (CNN). **STATE DEPARTMENT CONFIRMED PRIMARY: third round of US-facilitated Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington May 14-15, Lebanese delegation Simon Karam, Israeli delegation Ron Dermer, agenda includes 'complete disarmament of Hezbollah' + 'full restoration of Lebanese state authority' — material structural anchoring**. April-23-extended 3-week ceasefire expires May 15 as talks finish — choreographed. **Times of Israel May 14 liveblog leads with 'Iranian FM threatens UAE, claims Tehran knew of Netanyahu's visit to Abu Dhabi'** — Israeli press has operationally integrated the trilateral (Iran-UAE-Israel) framing as a single conflict surface.
